intent.summary: Receive Python-oriented tasks but emit AIDL directly.
intent.training_direction: This ruleset is the temporary bridge toward direct AIDL-native modeling and future AIDL-centric training.
intent.temporary_note: The skill plus structured-rules path is a prototype-stage integration strategy, not the intended final product.
intent.scaling_risk: If the ruleset becomes too large, too semantic, or too runtime-dependent, injecting it through prompts or skills may create token overhead and negative net benefit.
intent.current_constraint: At the current stage, syntax design must preserve bidirectional Python <-> AIDL conversion and one-to-one reversibility.

principles: Do not mirror Python syntax line by line.
Rewrite Python intent into the shortest stable AIDL form supported by the current translator.
Optimize for lower token output rather than human readability.
Do not introduce mainline syntax that breaks Python <-> AIDL reversibility at the current stage.

_flat_mode.python: left-aligned line + trailing digit for next-line indentation spaces
notes: Flat mode keeps every line left-aligned.
The trailing digit encodes the next line's indentation space count.
In the current Python path, the next indentation count should be even.
The current serializer keeps the last line suffix explicit for stable round-tripping.

F.python_shape: [x for x in seq if cond(x)]

FM.python_shape: [expr(x) for x in seq if cond(x)]

A.rewrite_hint: Use for existential checks over a sequence.

E.rewrite_hint: Use for universal checks over a sequence.

SFM.python_shape: sum(expr(x) for x in seq if cond(x))
SFM.rewrite_hint: Use instead of sum over filter-map.

CF.python_shape: sum(1 for x in seq if cond(x))
CF.rewrite_hint: Use instead of len(filter(...)) or counting comprehension results.

DFM.python_shape: {key(x): value(x) for x in seq if cond(x)}
DFM.rewrite_hint: Use for dict construction from a filtered sequence.

CO.python_shape: value if value is not None else fallback

KV.python_shape: {k_expr: v_expr for k, v in obj.items()}

KVF.python_shape: {k_expr: v_expr for k, v in obj.items() if cond}

ANYN.python_shape: any(it is None for it in (...))
ANYN.rewrite_hint: Use when any value may be None.

ALLNN.python_shape: all(it is not None for it in (...))
ALLNN.rewrite_hint: Use when all values must be non-None.

CNTNN.python_shape: sum(1 for it in (...) if it is not None)

preferred_rewrites.python: sum(expr(x) for x in seq if pred(x))
len([x for x in seq if pred(x)])
{k: v for k, v in obj.items()}
{k: v for k, v in obj.items() if cond}
value if value is not None else fallback
repeated is None / is not None checks

restrictions: Stay within the current Python translator subset.
Current syntax design must preserve bidirectional Python <-> AIDL reversibility.
Do not invent new macros in outputs.
If a construct is unsupported, use the narrowest possible py escape or explicitly state the limitation.
Do not emit Python and AIDL side by side unless explicitly requested.
